# AuraStack (AI-First Scaffold)

AuraStack is a Flask + React + RBAC scaffold optimized for AI-driven feature delivery.
Product managers provide only natural-language intent. Agents perform end-to-end implementation.

## Canonical Entry Points

- [Agent Rules](./AGENTS.md)
- [OpenAPI Source](./docs/apifox-full.openapi.json)
- [Apifox Push Script](./backend/scripts/import_openapi_to_apifox.py)
- [RBAC Seed Script](./backend/scripts/init_rbac_data.py)
- [Feature Verification Script](./backend/scripts/verify_feature.py)
- [Semi MCP Skill](./.agents/skills/semi-ui-skills/SKILL.md)

## Backend Structure

- App bootstrap: `backend/app/__init__.py`
- Global route assembler: `backend/app/router.py`
- Domain modules: `backend/app/admin/`, `backend/app/data_management/`
- Per-domain layers: `api/`, `service/`, `crud/`, `model/`, `schema/`
- Database migrations: `backend/migrations/`
- Engineering scripts: `backend/scripts/`
- Shared capabilities: `backend/common/`

## Tech Stack

- Backend: Flask 3 + Flask-SQLAlchemy + PostgreSQL
- Frontend: React 18 + Vite + React Router + Axios
- UI: Semi Design
- Import/Export: openpyxl/xlrd/xlwt

## MCP Docs Rule

- If MCP docs/skills are available for the task domain, read them first, then implement.
- For UI components, prefer `semi-mcp` + `semi-design-guide` skill as first reference.
- Keep final implementation aligned with existing project patterns.

## Frontend Structure

- Dynamic routes: `frontend/src/App.jsx`
- Layout/nav: `frontend/src/components/Layout/index.jsx`
- Module pages: `frontend/src/modules/**/pages/**/index.jsx`
- Module APIs: `frontend/src/modules/**/api/*.js`
- Shared import/export UI: `frontend/src/shared/components/import-export/*`
- Shared API client/utilities: `frontend/src/shared/api/*`, `frontend/src/shared/utils/*`

## Definition of Done

Any new module/feature should include:

1. Backend route implementation and registration.
2. Frontend page + API client + menu routing.
3. RBAC menu/button permissions and seed updates.
4. Run `python3 backend/scripts/init_rbac_data.py --incremental` to sync menus and refresh super-admin permissions.
5. Migration script(s) when schema changes.
6. OpenAPI JSON update and optional Apifox push.
7. Build/compile verification.
8. If import/export is implemented: follow the existing import/export pattern already used in this project.
9. If migration/schema changed: explicitly tell user to run `flask db upgrade -d backend/migrations`.
10. Every delivery must include a concise "next steps" command checklist for the user.
